req.title: Manager views team
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a team through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
preconditions: authenticated
role:manager
team module is enabled

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated manager can view a team via GET /api/teams, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and HTTP 200/201 responses on success. Error paths include 400 (bad request), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (forbidden role). I apply EP to cover the valid manager role partition, NEG for unauthenticated and forbidden-role partitions, BVA for the boundary of a team with the minimum required fields, DT for the interaction of role x authentication x team-module flags, and STC for the state transition from 'team exists' to 'team viewed with audit written'. The suite uses pytest with a shared requests session fixture and a lightweight audit-check helper placeholder.

tests.name: test_manager_views_team_returns_200_and_audit_written
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Team module is enabled
At least one team exists with a known team_id
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_auth_token>
Capture response status code, body, and entity id
Query audit log via <get_latest_audit_record> for the team_id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the view action on team_id
trace: R-WEB-012: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_manager_views_team_returns_200_and_audit_written():
    token = <get_auth_token>(role="manager")
    team_id = <get_existing_team_id>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{team_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id"), "Response body must contain a non-empty entity id"

    audit = <get_latest_audit_record>(entity_id=team_id, action="view")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful view"
    assert audit["entity_id"] == str(team_id)

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401
preconditions: No Authorization header is provided
Team module is enabled
At least one team exists with a known team_id
actions: Send GET /api/teams/{team_id} with no Authorization header
expected: No audit record is written for this attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_unauthenticated_request_returns_401():
    team_id = <get_existing_team_id>()

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{team_id}")

    assert resp.status_code == 401, (
        f"Expected 401 for unauthenticated request, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    audit = <get_latest_audit_record>(entity_id=team_id, action="view")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated access"

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not manager)
Team module is enabled
At least one team exists with a known team_id
actions: Obtain a valid auth token for role 'employee' via <get_auth_token>
expected: No audit record is written for this attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_forbidden_role_returns_403():
    token = <get_auth_token>(role="employee")
    team_id = <get_existing_team_id>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{team_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for forbidden role, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    audit = <get_latest_audit_record>(entity_id=team_id, action="view")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written for forbidden role access"

tests.name: test_manager_views_team_with_minimum_mandatory_fields_only
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Team module is enabled
A team exists containing only the minimum set of mandatory fields
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_auth_token>
Resolve the team_id for the minimum-field team via <get_min_field_team_id>
Capture response status code and body
expected: Response body contains all mandatory fields with non-null values
Response body entity id is non-empty
Audit record is written for the view action
trace: R-WEB-012: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-012: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"
MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["id", "name", "manager_id"]

def test_manager_views_team_with_minimum_mandatory_fields_only():
    token = <get_auth_token>(role="manager")
    team_id = <get_min_field_team_id>()
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}

    resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{team_id}", headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    for field in MANDATORY_FIELDS:
        assert body.get(field) not in (None, ""), (
            f"Mandatory field '{field}' must be present and non-null"
        )
    audit = <get_latest_audit_record>(entity_id=team_id, action="view")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written for minimum-field team view"

tests.name: test_manager_views_team_state_transition_from_created_to_viewed
preconditions: User is authenticated as a manager
Team module is enabled
No team with the test name exists (clean state)
actions: Obtain a valid manager auth token via <get_auth_token>
Create a new team via POST /api/teams to reach 'team exists' state
Send GET /api/teams/{new_team_id} to trigger the 'view' transition
Query audit log for the view event
expected: GET response body id matches the created team id
Audit record for 'view' action exists after GET
trace: R-WEB-012: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://hr-app/api"

def test_manager_views_team_state_transition_from_created_to_viewed():
    token = <get_auth_token>(role="manager")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    payload = <build_valid_team_payload>(name="stc-team-r-web-012")

    create_resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/teams", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    new_team_id = create_resp.json()["id"]

    view_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/teams/{new_team_id}", headers=headers)
    assert view_resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 200 or 201 on view, got {view_resp.status_code}"
    )
    assert view_resp.json()["id"] == new_team_id

    audit = <get_latest_audit_record>(entity_id=new_team_id, action="view")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist after state transition to viewed"

    <cleanup_team>(team_id=new_team_id, headers=headers)

tests.name: test_decision_table_role_auth_module_combinations
preconditions: Team module can be toggled via <set_team_module_enabled>
Multiple user roles are available: manager, employee, unauthenticated
At least one team exists
actions: For each combination of (role, authenticated, module_enabled), send GET /api/teams/{team_id}
Capture status code for each combination
trace: R-WEB-012: precondition team module is enabled
